compiled_state.title: ticket_graph cache key omits mtime (same as 1d76): same-size event rewrite serves stale graph via deps/ready/next-batch
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Sibling of 1d76-b6d1 (reducer cache), found auditing for that antipattern.

## Antipattern
Cache key built from filename+size only, so an equal-byte-length in-place rewrite (git checkout/rebase of the tickets branch, fsck-recover cherry-pick) is undetected -> stale cached value served.

## Location (independent copy, NOT fixed by the 1d76 reducer fix)
src/rebar/_engine/ticket_graph/_cache.py:_compute_cache_key — uses os.path.getsize -> f"{name}:{size}". Docstring even says "Uses the same dir_hash method as the reducer: filename + file size." The reducer copy (ticket_reducer/_cache.py) was fixed to include st_mtime_ns; this graph-cache copy was not. The .graph-cache.json backs deps / ready / next-batch (agent scheduling surface).

## Experiment (confirmed)
Built a tracker dir with one CREATE event, computed _compute_cache_key, rewrote the event same-byte-length (AAAA->BBBB), recomputed: KEY UNCHANGED -> cache hit -> stale graph. (Mirrors the reducer repro.)

## Fix
Apply the same fix as 1d76: fold st_mtime_ns into the per-file hash part (single os.stat). Consider factoring the shared dir-hash so the two caches cannot diverge again.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] same-size rewrite invalidates the graph cache (RED test first)
- [ ] cache still hits on unchanged dirs (no read-path regression)
- [ ] dedupe with the reducer dir-hash or document why separate
